name: TPN-101
summary: Clinical-stage reverse transcriptase inhibitor being repurposed by the ARPA-H PROSPR program to test whether suppressing retrotransposon-linked inflammation can improve aging-related outcomes.
mechanism_summary: Reverse transcriptase inhibitor positioned for LINE1-linked reverse-transcription biology and downstream innate immune activation, distinct from broad antiretroviral use and from downstream cytokine blockade.

open_questions: Whether the ARPA-H-supported healthy-aging program opens a registry and reports prespecified target-engagement and intrinsic-capacity endpoints
Whether prior neurodegeneration safety or biomarker experience generalizes to healthy older adults
Whether TPN-101 directly suppresses LINE1 or retrotransposon cDNA activity in aging-relevant human tissues
